settings.title: Meet Dani, the Hands Behind Your Morning Flat White
settings.excerpt: Our head barista on latte art, early starts, and why the second cup is always better.
settings.body:
If you have been in before nine, you have probably been served by Dani. She has been pulling shots with us for three years and runs the bar most mornings.

Dani came to coffee from a kitchen background, which shows in how she thinks about balance and consistency. She is the reason our milk is steamed the same way every single time.

From the kitchen to the bar

Before she started with us, Dani spent years on a restaurant line, where timing and repetition are everything. That training carried straight over to the espresso machine. She treats a flat white the way a good cook treats a sauce: taste, adjust, and never send out something that is not right. When a shot runs a touch fast, she catches it before it reaches you and starts again.

Ask her what she actually likes to drink and the answer surprises people. On a day off she usually orders the second cup first, because the morning rush rarely lets her sit with one. Her standards for the bar come from caring about that quiet second cup as much as the first.

A few things Dani swears by

  • Wipe the steam wand every time, no exceptions.
  • Warm the cup before the espresso ever lands in it.
  • The second pour of the day is almost always the best one.

Ask her about single-origin espresso if you have a minute, just maybe not during the morning rush. She is happy to talk through what is on bar, and she has strong opinions she will share gladly once the line has thinned out.
